revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"
Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23392883474|time triggered pipeline]]

[[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=En_dash%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Distinguish|Template:Em dash}} {{documentation subpage}} {{Short description|Template to output an n-dash character}} {{High-use}} {{COinS safe}} {{Template shortcuts|ndash|endash|nsndns}} This template produces an '''[[:en:En dash|En dash]]''' (–), which is longer than a [[:en:Hyphen|Hyphen]] (-) but shorter than an [[:en:em dash|em dash]] (—). ==Usage== The primary uses of an en dash are: * To indicate a range of numbers or dates (e.g. {{xt|1914{{en dash}}1918}}) * To show a relationship between things (e.g. {{xt|a New York{{en dash}}London flight}}) For further detail on dash usage, see [[:en:MOS:DASH|MOS:DASH]]. ==TemplateData== {{Format TemplateData|JSON={"params":{},"description": "This template produces an [[:en:En dash|En dash]] (–) character. It has the same effect as the HTML escape sequence –." } }} Syntax:{{en dash}} == See also == * {{tl|Non breaking en dash}} * {{tl|Spaced en dash space}} * {{tl|Spaced en dash}} * {{tl|Em dash}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24931715792|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Distinguish|Template:Em dash}} {{documentation subpage}} {{Short description|Template to output an n-dash character}} {{High-use}} {{COinS safe}} {{Template shortcuts|ndash|endash|nsndns}} This template produces an '''[[:en:En dash|En dash]]''' (–), which is longer than a [[:en:Hyphen|Hyphen]] (-) but shorter than an [[:en:em dash|em dash]] (—). ==Usage== The primary uses of an en dash are: * To indicate a range of numbers or dates (e.g. {{xt|1914{{en dash}}1918}}) * To show a relationship between things (e.g. {{xt|a New York{{en dash}}London flight}}) For further detail on dash usage, see [[:en:MOS:DASH|MOS:DASH]]. ==TemplateData== {{Format TemplateData|JSON={"params":{},"description": "This template produces an [[:en:En dash|En dash]] (–) character. It has the same effect as the HTML escape sequence –." } }} Syntax:{{en dash}} == See also == * {{tl|Non breaking en dash}} * {{tl|Spaced en dash space}} * {{tl|Spaced en dash}} * {{tl|Em dash}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
